ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general baked rice dish class; no specific entry for Nanyang curry baked rice with Lion's Mane mushroom patty. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Nanyang curry baked rice. A0CKP (rice dish, mixed) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Curry and mushroom patty components are not individually coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for this composite baked rice dish. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for restaurant-style fusion rice dishes.

curry spices (turmeric, cumin, coriander, ginger, garlic, chilli)
allergens: gluten (possible — curry sauce may contain wheat-based thickeners)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of baked rice with Nanyang curry and Lion's Mane mushroom cheese patty, approximately 500 g, as typically served in a Singaporean café or casual dining setting.

nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for BR8 Baked Rice Nanyang Curry Lion's Mane Cheeeezy Patty exists in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Nutrient values are estimated from the input record (provenance: GrabFood menu listing, confidence 0.7, ai_generated) and cross-referenced against HPB Foodo entries for baked rice dishes with curry sauce and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for Lion's Mane mushroom (FDC ID 2346406) and mozzarella/cheddar cheese (FDC ID 173420). The input per-100g values are retained as the best available estimate given the composite nature of the dish. Confidence is set to medium. HPB Foodo is the primary regional source; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is used as a secondary global source for component cross-reference.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate for mixed-grain rice dish with curry sauce
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nanyang curry baked rice with Lion's Mane mushroom patty. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate derived from the University of Sydney GI Database (glycemicindex.com) entry for mixed-grain rice dishes with curry sauce, which reports GI values in the range of 50–60 for mixed-grain rice preparations with fat- and protein-containing sauces. The presence of dietary fibre at 2.22 g/100g, coconut milk fat, and cheese protein moderates the GI relative to plain white rice (GI ~72). A conservative mid-range estimate of 56 is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 72.2) / 100 = 40.4, based on 500 g serving with 14.44 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Source: University of Sydney GI Database, accessed via glycemicindex.com, and Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 244 mg/100g; estimated 1,222 mg per 500 g serving, approaching 53% of the 2,300 mg daily limit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced curry sauce and cheese topping to lower sodium intake, and limit portion size to no more than 300 g per meal to keep sodium below 730 mg per eating occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate at 14.44 g/100g with dietary fibre at 2.22 g/100g (below the 3 g/100g threshold); estimated GI of 56 (medium) yields a high glycemic load of 40.4 per 500 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 250–300 g, request a higher proportion of mixed grains (brown rice, quinoa, red rice) to increase fibre above 3 g/100g, and avoid additional curry sauce to reduce both glycemic load and saturated fat from coconut milk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100g from coconut milk and cheese; trans fat at 0.04 g/100g; sodium at 244 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request reduced cheese and curry sauce to lower saturated fat and sodium, and limit portion to 300 g per meal, as a full 500 g serving provides approximately 20 g saturated fat, which approaches the recommended daily limit of 20 g for CVD risk management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100g is below the 5 g/100g RA threshold; Lion's Mane mushroom provides beta-glucans with anti-inflammatory properties; curry spices (turmeric, cumin, ginger, garlic) are evidence-based anti-inflammatory compounds
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may consume this dish in moderation, but should monitor response to nightshade ingredients (chilli, bell pepper) and request reduced cheese and coconut milk to keep saturated fat below 5 g/100g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: BR8 Baked Rice with Nanyang Curry and Lion's Mane Mushroom Cheese Patty is a Singaporean fusion baked rice dish served in a café or casual dining setting. The dish consists of a base of rice — typically a mixed-grain blend of white, brown, and red rice — cooked with a Nanyang-style curry sauce made from coconut milk, turmeric, cumin, coriander, ginger, garlic, and chilli. A Lion's Mane mushroom patty is placed on top of the rice, and the entire dish is covered with a generous layer of melted cheese before being baked in an oven until golden and crisp. The result is a dish with a rich, aromatic curry flavour, a creamy and slightly spicy sauce, a meaty-textured mushroom patty, and a golden cheese crust. The dish is vegetarian. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 151 kcal, 6.2 g protein, 7.8 g fat (4.0 g saturated), 14.4 g carbohydrate, 2.2 g dietary fibre, and 244 mg sodium. The estimated glycemic index is 56 (medium), yielding a high glycemic load of approximately 40 per standard 500 g serving. Clinically, the dish is moderate concern for hypertension (high sodium from curry sauce and cheese), Type 2 Diabetes (medium GI, high GL per serving, fibre below 3 g/100g threshold), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat from coconut milk and cheese). Lion's Mane mushroom contributes beta-glucans with immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory properties. Curry spices including turmeric and ginger provide evidence-based anti-inflammatory phytochemicals. The dish is best consumed in a reduced portion (250–300 g) by individuals managing cardiometabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this dish exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Nutrient values are derived from the input GrabFood menu record (confidence 0.7, provenance ai_generated) and cross-referenced against HPB Foodo baked rice dish entries and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for Lion's Mane mushroom and cheese. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed-grain rice with curry sauce. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite nature of the dish and the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite baked rice dish is not available in HPB Foodo Singapore or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for this specific dish is not reported in the input record and is not available from an approved source for this exact composite preparation as of 2024. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the input record and are not available from an approved source for this specific composite dish preparation as of 2024; confirmed unavailable in HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 4. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 in the input record; this is plausible for a vegetarian dish with no egg or meat components, though cheese typically contributes trace cholesterol — this value is retained from the source record with low confidence and is treated as confirmed zero per the input source. 5. trans_fat_g at 0.04 g/100g is a trace amount, consistent with small quantities from dairy-derived cheese. 6. The input record contained two duplicate portion entries both labelled '1 plate' with different weights (550 g and 450 g); a consolidated estimate of 500 g is used as the serving size. 7. The input cultural ethnic_tags ('Ramen', 'Pizza', 'Pasta') are inconsistent with the dish identity and have been corrected to reflect Singaporean Fusion cuisine. 8. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database; actual GI may vary depending on the rice blend composition and curry sauce formulation used by the specific restaurant. 9. Nutrient source: HPB Foodo (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/) is the primary approved regional source; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/) is used as the secondary approved global source for component cross-reference. No forbidden sources are used.
